event.title: US-Iran Ceasefire Extension Framework: Negotiations Near Deal but No Final Agreement
event.summary: US and Iranian negotiators reached a tentative framework to extend their ceasefire by 60 days and launch nuclear talks, but as of late May 2026 the deal had not been approved by either Donald Trump or Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei. Both sides continued to accuse each other of ceasefire violations while publicly disputing the terms Trump described.

claims.text: US and Iranian negotiators reached a tentative framework agreement (described as a memorandum of understanding) to extend the ceasefire for 60 days.
claims.rationale: Multiple outlets across center and center-left bias tiers consistently reported that negotiators from both sides agreed on a 60-day ceasefire extension framework, satisfying cross-spectrum confirmation.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC reports US officials confirmed a framework MOU was agreed, pending Trump and Iran leadership approval
Euronews reports negotiators from both sides reached agreement on a 60-day deal
Euronews describes it as a 'tentative framework agreement reached on Thursday'
The Guardian calls it a 'reported memorandum of understanding' reached with Pakistani and Qatari mediators
Reuters reports a deal was reached needing Trump's final approval

claims.text: The framework had not yet been approved by Donald Trump as of late May 2026.
claims.rationale: Sources spanning center, center-left, and right all reported that Trump had not yet signed off on the agreement, confirming this across the political spectrum.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC reports Trump was briefed but 'did not immediately sign off' and would take 'a couple of days to consider it'
The Guardian reports the New York Times cited a senior official saying Trump 'had not made a decision'
Euronews states the meeting concluded without a decision
Haaretz headlines that Trump 'did not reach a decision on an Iran deal'

claims.text: The framework had not yet been confirmed or approved by Iran's leadership.
claims.rationale: Both center and center-left outlets cited Iranian official statements and Iran-aligned news agencies stating no final agreement had been reached, providing cross-spectrum confirmation.

framing_variants.characterization: Iran's semi-official Tasnim news agency reported the framework 'had not been finalised or confirmed'
BBC notes the framework 'has not been confirmed by Iran'
Iranian foreign ministry spokesperson Baqaei stated no final agreement had been reached
Euronews cites Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Baghaei saying 'no deal had yet been finalised'

claims.text: The framework included provisions for Iran to remove all mines from the Strait of Hormuz within 30 days.
claims.rationale: Multiple center and center-left outlets reported this as a framework provision; however, Iran's Fars news agency (cited in The Guardian) disputed that the agreement included provisions for opening the strait without fees or destroying nuclear material, indicating Iranian contestation of the terms as described.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC reports Iran would be given 30 days to remove mines from the Strait of Hormuz
Euronews lists 'Iran removing all mines from the strait within 30 days' as a draft term
Trump stated the deal would 'eliminate mines in the waterway'; Iranian Fars agency disputed the terms as a 'mixture of truth and lies'

claims.text: The framework included provisions for unrestricted passage through the Strait of Hormuz.
claims.rationale: US-side sources and Trump's own statements described unrestricted, toll-free passage as a deal requirement; Iranian sources including Fars news agency disputed this, saying the agreement did not include provisions for opening the strait without fees.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC reports the framework 'could allow unrestricted passage through the Strait of Hormuz'

Trump said the deal would require Iran to 'open the strait of Hormuz for all traffic without tolls'; Iran's Fars agency said the agreement 'did not include provisions for the opening of the strait of Hormuz without fees'

claims.text: The framework included a proportional lifting of the US naval blockade as commercial shipping resumes, and US sanctions waivers to allow Iran to sell oil.
claims.rationale: Center and center-left outlets consistently reported these as framework provisions, providing cross-spectrum corroboration.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC reports 'the US would lift its blockade' and 'issue sanction waivers to allow Iran to resume selling oil'
Euronews lists 'a proportional lifting of the US naval blockade as commercial shipping resumes' and 'US sanctions waivers to allow Iran to sell oil'
Trump said he would 'lift the US naval blockade against Iran'

claims.text: Nuclear negotiations on Iran's programme would begin during the 60-day ceasefire extension window.
claims.rationale: Multiple center and center-left outlets reported nuclear talks as part of the framework; however, Iran's foreign ministry spokesperson stated negotiators were 'not currently discussing the specifics of Iran's nuclear programme,' introducing contestation on whether nuclear talks were part of the current scope.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC describes the framework as launching 'talks on the future of Iran's nuclear programme'
Euronews states 'nuclear negotiations would begin during the 60-day window' with Iran's enriched uranium stockpile 'the first issue on the agenda'
Euronews also reports Iranian Foreign Ministry said negotiators were 'not currently discussing the specifics of Iran's nuclear programme'

claims.text: US Vice President JD Vance stated that the US and Iran were very close to a deal but had not yet reached one, and that negotiators were still working out sticking points including the question of uranium enrichment.
claims.rationale: Center and center-left outlets all attributed substantially identical statements to Vance, confirming the claim across bias tiers.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC quotes Vance: 'We're not there yet, but we're very close,' with sticking points including 'the question of enrichment'
Euronews reports Vance said talks were making 'good progress'
The Guardian says Vance 'hinted on Thursday night that an agreement was close'

claims.text: Vance stated the US believed Iran was negotiating in good faith.
claims.rationale: Only BBC reported this specific statement from Vance; it is not corroborated by other outlets.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC quotes Vance saying 'the US believed the Iranians were negotiating in good faith'

claims.text: Trump posted on Truth Social outlining his conditions for a deal, including Iran never having a nuclear weapon, immediate opening of the Strait of Hormuz with no tolls, elimination of water mines, and US access to destroy highly enriched uranium.
claims.rationale: Both The Guardian (center-left) and Euronews (center) reported Trump's Truth Social post and its specific conditions, confirming this across bias tiers.

framing_variants.characterization: The Guardian reports Trump said Iran 'must agree that they will never have a Nuclear Weapon or Bomb' and must open the strait 'without tolls'
Euronews summarizes Trump's stated red lines including no nuclear weapon, open Hormuz with no tolls, and termination of mines

claims.text: Trump claimed he could approve a deal by Friday that would include major concessions from Tehran, including opening of the Strait of Hormuz and elimination of Iran's nuclear programme.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian specifically framed Trump's statement as claiming he could 'approve' the deal 'by Friday' with those specific listed terms as framed in that article.

framing_variants.characterization: The Guardian reports Trump 'claimed he could approve an Iran peace deal on Friday that contains major concessions from Tehran'

claims.text: Trump characterizes the deal as requiring elimination of Iran's nuclear programme; Iranian officials characterize nuclear renunciation as an unjustified US imposition and state Iran has no intention of transferring its uranium stockpile.
claims.rationale: Trump and US officials demanded Iran never develop nuclear weapons and allow destruction of enriched uranium; Iranian parliamentary officials publicly rejected committing to America on nuclear matters and denied uranium transfer plans, representing a direct framing dispute.

framing_variants.characterization: Trump says deal would allow the US to 'unearth and destroy highly enriched uranium'; Iranian official Rezaei asks 'Why should we commit to America that we will not build a nuclear weapon?' and Azizi denies uranium transfer plans

Trump insists on 'a guarantee that Tehran would never be able to develop nuclear weapons'; Iranian Foreign Ministry says negotiators are 'not currently discussing the specifics of Iran's nuclear programme'
BBC notes 'The US has long demanded that Iran stop producing highly enriched uranium' while 'Iran insists its nuclear programme is entirely peaceful'

claims.text: Iran's Fars news agency described Trump's account of the deal terms as a 'mixture of truth and lies,' specifically disputing provisions on opening the strait without fees and destruction of nuclear material.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian reported this specific characterization from Iran's Fars news agency.

framing_variants.characterization: The Guardian quotes Fars saying Trump published 'a mixture of truth and lies' and the agreement did not include opening the strait without fees or destruction of nuclear material

claims.text: Iran's Tasnim news agency, described as IRGC-affiliated, stated that the final text of the agreement had not been completed or approved.
claims.rationale: BBC, Euronews, and The Guardian all cited Tasnim's statement that no final agreement had been reached, confirming this across center and center-left outlets.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC: 'Iran's semi-official Tasnim news agency reported it had not been finalised or confirmed'
Euronews describes Tasnim as 'IRGC-affiliated' and quotes a source saying the final text 'has not yet been completed or approved'
The Guardian: Tasnim said 'no final understanding had been reached' and Trump's post was 'in line with his usual pattern of making unilateral and egotistical statements'

claims.text: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei stated that negotiations were ongoing but no final agreement had been reached.
claims.rationale: Both The Guardian (center-left) and Euronews (center) reported Baqaei's statement confirming no final deal across bias tiers.

framing_variants.characterization: The Guardian quotes Baqaei: 'exchanges of messages are continuing, but no final agreement has been reached yet'
Euronews reports Baghaei stated 'no deal had yet been finalised' and negotiators were focused on ending the war, not nuclear specifics

claims.text: On Wednesday, Iranian state media reported elements of what it described as an unofficial draft 14-point memorandum of understanding, which the White House called a 'complete fabrication.'
claims.rationale: Both BBC articles reported both the Iranian state media leak and the White House denial, confirming the episode across center-rated outlets.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC reports the draft included lifting of the naval blockade, US force withdrawal and Iran/Oman control of Hormuz; White House called it a 'complete fabrication'
BBC describes the White House statement as 'terse' and the draft provisions in detail

claims.text: The reported draft 14-point MOU included lifting Washington's naval blockade of Iranian ports, withdrawal of US forces from the vicinity of Iran, and restoration of non-military traffic through the Strait of Hormuz with Iran and Oman managing vessel routing.
claims.rationale: Both BBC articles reported the same draft provisions; both are center-rated outlets, making this corroborated rather than cross-spectrum agreed.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC lists draft provisions including lifting of blockade, US force withdrawal, and Iran/Oman control of Hormuz traffic
BBC provides the same set of provisions in similar detail

claims.text: Trump met with senior advisers for approximately two hours in the White House Situation Room without reaching a decision on the Iran deal.
claims.rationale: Both The Guardian (center-left) and Euronews (center) reported the Situation Room meeting's duration and inconclusive outcome.

framing_variants.characterization: The Guardian reports 'Trump emerged from the White House situation room after spending more than two hours with senior aides'
Euronews reports 'Trump met with advisers for two hours in the White House Situation Room on Friday' and 'the meeting concluded without a decision'

claims.text: A White House official stated that Trump would only make a deal satisfying his red lines, including Iran never possessing a nuclear weapon.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian specifically attributed this statement to a White House official in this form.

framing_variants.characterization: The Guardian quotes White House official: 'President Trump will only make a deal that is good for America and satisfies his red lines' and 'Iran can never possess a nuclear weapon'

claims.text: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent declined to confirm that a deal had been reached, saying 'it is all going to be the president's decision.'
claims.rationale: BBC (center) and Arutz Sheva (right) both reported Bessent's deferral to Trump's decision, but both are relatively close in the spectrum; while they differ in bias tier, the claim is supported by two distinct outlets.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC quotes Bessent: 'It's always a mistake to get out ahead of the president' and 'it is all going to be the president's decision'
Arutz Sheva frames the Treasury Secretary as acknowledging the deal's status depends on Trump's intentions

claims.text: Pakistan is acting as a key mediator in US-Iran negotiations, with Foreign Minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar meeting US Secretary of State Marco Rubio in Washington.
claims.rationale: Euronews (center) and The Guardian (center-left) both reported Pakistan's mediating role and the Rubio-Dar meeting, confirming across bias tiers.

framing_variants.characterization: Euronews: 'Pakistan's Foreign Minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar, acting as a mediator between Tehran and Washington, will meet his US counterpart Marco Rubio in Washington on Friday'
The Guardian reports the meeting 'ended without any public comment on the negotiations'
BBC describes the diplomatic effort as 'spearheaded by Pakistan'

claims.text: Qatar also played a role as mediator alongside Pakistan in the ceasefire negotiations.
claims.rationale: Only The Guardian mentioned Qatar as a co-mediator alongside Pakistan; other outlets referenced only Pakistan.

framing_variants.characterization: The Guardian mentions 'Pakistani and Qatari mediators' in describing the MOU's origins

claims.text: Iran and the US have accused each other of violating the ceasefire since it came into effect.
claims.rationale: BBC, Euronews, and another Euronews article all reported mutual ceasefire violation accusations, confirming across center and center-left bias tiers.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC: 'both Iran and the US have accused each other of violating the fragile ceasefire in the past few days'
Euronews reports 'sporadic exchange of fire between Iran and the US, particularly along Iran's southern coast in the Persian Gulf'
Euronews: 'Both Iran and US have accused each other of violating the ceasefire'

claims.text: Iran's IRGC claimed it targeted a US air base in the region; US Central Command denied any US aircraft were shot down and said all US air assets were accounted for.
claims.rationale: Both the IRGC's claim of attacking a US base and Iranian state media's claim of downing a US aircraft were directly contradicted by US Central Command's denial; these are treated as a contested claim reflecting incompatible accounts from both sides.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC: IRGC 'said it targeted a US base in the region on Thursday'; Iranian state media 'reported that the country's forces had taken down a US aircraft, possibly a drone'; CENTCOM said 'No US aircraft were shot down'
BBC: 'Its Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) then said it had attacked an American air base'; 'a ballistic missile had been intercepted over Kuwait'
Euronews: Tehran 'claimed to have downed a US aircraft near Bushehr'; CENTCOM denied the claim

claims.text: The US conducted strikes on southern Iran, including a strike targeting a site near Bandar Abbas airport.
claims.rationale: BBC and Euronews both reported US strikes in southern Iran including near Bandar Abbas, confirming this across center and center-left outlets.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC reports 'fresh US strikes on southern Iran overnight'
BBC describes US Central Command targeting 'a ground control site' in Bandar Abbas

Euronews reports Tehran's Friday prayers leader confirmed the strike near Bandar Abbas airport 'caused neither human casualties nor material damage' but was 'an infringement on Iranian airspace and territory'

claims.text: The US said it shot down five Iranian drones that posed a threat around the Strait of Hormuz.
claims.rationale: Only BBC reported this specific US claim about shooting down five Iranian drones near the Strait of Hormuz.

framing_variants.characterization: BBC: 'The US said on Thursday it had shot down five Iranian drones which posed a threat around the Strait of Hormuz'

paragraphs.text: US and Iranian negotiators reached a tentative framework agreement in late May 2026 to extend their ceasefire for 60 days, according to multiple reports across the political spectrum. The framework, described as a memorandum of understanding, had not been approved by either US President Donald Trump or Iran's leadership as of the end of May. Both sides continued negotiating while publicly disputing the specific terms of the proposed deal.

The framework called for Iran to remove all mines from the Strait of Hormuz within 30 days, according to center and center-left outlets. In exchange, the US would proportionally lift its naval blockade as commercial shipping resumed and provide sanctions waivers allowing Iran to sell oil. Nuclear negotiations on Iran's programme would begin during the 60-day extension window.

US Vice President JD Vance stated that the US and Iran were "very close to a deal" but had not yet reached one, and that negotiators were still working out sticking points including the question of uranium enrichment. Trump met with senior advisers for approximately two hours in the White House Situation Room without reaching a decision on the Iran deal. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent declined to confirm a deal had been reached, saying "it is all going to be the president's decision."

Pakistan acted as a key mediator in the negotiations, with Foreign Minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar meeting US Secretary of State Marco Rubio in Washington. Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei stated that negotiations were ongoing but no final agreement had been reached. Iran's Tasnim news agency, described as affiliated with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, similarly stated that the final text of the agreement had not been completed or approved.

Trump posted on Truth Social outlining his conditions for a deal, including Iran never having a nuclear weapon, immediate opening of the Strait of Hormuz with no tolls, elimination of water mines, and US access to destroy highly enriched uranium. On Wednesday, Iranian state media reported elements of what it described as an unofficial draft 14-point memorandum of understanding, which the White House called a "complete fabrication." The reported draft included lifting Washington's naval blockade of Iranian ports, withdrawal of US forces from the vicinity of Iran, and restoration of non-military traffic through the Strait of Hormuz with Iran and Oman managing vessel routing.

Trump and Iranian officials described the framework's nuclear provisions in fundamentally incompatible terms. Trump characterized the deal as requiring elimination of Iran's nuclear programme and allowing US access to destroy enriched uranium. Iranian parliamentary officials publicly rejected committing to America on nuclear matters and denied any intention of transferring Iran's uranium stockpile. Iran's foreign ministry spokesperson stated negotiators were "not currently discussing the specifics of Iran's nuclear programme," while multiple US-aligned sources reported nuclear talks as part of the framework.

A similar dispute emerged over passage through the Strait of Hormuz. US-side sources and Trump's own statements described unrestricted, toll-free passage as a deal requirement. However, Iran's Fars news agency described Trump's account of the deal terms as a "mixture of truth and lies," specifically disputing provisions on opening the strait without fees and destruction of nuclear material.

Iran and the US have accused each other of violating the ceasefire since it came into effect. The US conducted strikes on southern Iran, including a strike targeting a site near Bandar Abbas airport. The US said it shot down five Iranian drones that posed a threat around the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's IRGC claimed it targeted a US air base in the region and Iranian state media claimed to have downed a US aircraft; US Central Command denied any US aircraft were shot down and said all US air assets were accounted for.
Center-rated outlets reported that Vice President Vance stated the US believed Iran was negotiating in good faith, despite the ongoing disputes over terms and mutual ceasefire violation accusations.

The Guardian reported that Trump claimed he could approve a deal by Friday that would include major concessions from Tehran, including opening of the Strait of Hormuz and elimination of Iran's nuclear programme. A White House official told the same outlet that Trump would only make a deal satisfying his red lines, including Iran never possessing a nuclear weapon.
The Guardian also reported that Qatar played a role as mediator alongside Pakistan in the ceasefire negotiations, though other outlets mentioned only Pakistan's involvement.
